name: John Coltrane
abstract: John William Coltrane (September 23, 1926 – July 17, 1967) was an American jazz saxophonist, bandleader, and composer. He is among the most influential and acclaimed figures in the history of jazz and 20th-century music.

concerts.label: Horizons in Jazz featuring Dizzy Gillespie, George Shearing, and Others
Midnight Performance: Thanksgiving Jazz at Carnegie Hall
New Year's Eve Jazz at Carnegie Hall featuring John Coltrane, Thelonious Monk, Sonny Rollins, Nina Simone and George Crater

about_quotes.speaker: Allan Vaché
about_quotes.evidence: I spent a lot of time as a kid listening to saxophone players as much as I listened to clarinet players because I mean it’s amazing the things you can learn listening to Lester Young or John Coltrane or Charlie Parker or Coleman Hawkins or any one of these guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Allan Vaché interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 15, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Karolina Strassmayer
about_quotes.evidence: I bought everything that had a saxophone on it. And I didn’t know, you know I had everything from of course Charlie Parker, Phil Woods, Ornette Coleman, then Grover Washington Jr., Coltrane
about_quotes.doc_title: Karolina Strassmayer interviewed by Monk Rowe, May 25, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Smulyan
about_quotes.evidence: If you listen to, just the tenor saxophone, right? John Coltrane, Johnny Griffin, Gene Ammons... they all play the tenor saxophone but they all have — ... I could go on all day

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Robinson
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve seen in print the term traditional jazz as applied to hard bop... you would never hear John Coltrane... hear John Coltrane’s music described as trad jazz.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Robinson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2007
